settings.digest_help: Receive a periodic summary of new discussions, active threads, and new members.
settings.save_error: Could not save your preference. Please try again.

onboarding.modal_body: Get a periodic digest of the best discussions, new members, and what's been happening on the forum — delivered straight to your inbox.

settings.limit_new_help: Maximum number of new discussions to include in each digest.
settings.limit_hot_help: Maximum number of active (hot) discussions to include in each digest.
settings.limit_unread_help: Maximum number of unread discussions to surface per recipient.
settings.limit_leaderboard_help: Number of leaderboard entries to show in the digest (default 10, min 3). Requires huseyinfiliz-leaderboard.
settings.limit_badges_help: Maximum number of recent badge earners to list in the digest (default 5, min 3). Requires fof/badges.
settings.limit_pickem_help: Maximum number of upcoming matches, recent results, and leaderboard entries to show in the digest (default 5, min 3). Requires huseyinfiliz/pickem.
settings.enable_picks_help: Show upcoming games, recent results, and the picks leaderboard in the digest. Requires ernestdefoe/picks.
settings.limit_picks_label: CFB Picks — max items per section
settings.limit_picks_help: Maximum number of upcoming games, recent results, and leaderboard entries to show in the digest (default 5, min 3). Requires ernestdefoe/picks.
settings.picks_leaderboard_scope_help: Which leaderboard to show: the most recently completed week, the current season, or all-time standings.
settings.limit_gamepedia_help: Maximum number of games to show in the Most Discussed and Newly Added sections (default 5, min 3). Requires huseyinfiliz/gamepedia.
settings.limit_resofire_gamepedia_label: Resofire Gamepedia — max games per section
settings.limit_resofire_gamepedia_help: Maximum number of games to show in the Most Discussed, Newly Added, and Top Genres sections (default 5, min 3). Requires resofire/gamepedia.
settings.limit_favorites_help: Maximum number of favorite discussions to show, ranked by likes/reactions during the period (default 6, min 3). Set to 0 to disable. Uses fof/reactions if enabled, otherwise flarum/likes.
settings.enable_awards_help: Show active and upcoming awards in the digest. Requires huseyinfiliz/awards.
settings.queue_chunk_size_help: Number of users dispatched per scheduler minute (default 200, min 50, max 10000). Small forums: 200–500. Large forums on dedicated servers: 1000–10000. Higher values finish faster but use more memory per cycle.
settings.queue_delay_help: Seconds before queued jobs become available to workers (default 0). In window mode this is rarely needed — the window itself spreads load across time. Only useful if using the two-phase digest:enqueue command on very large forums.
settings.queue_tries_help: How many times a failed digest job will be retried before being moved to the failed jobs table (default 3). Retries use exponential backoff: 30s, 60s, 120s. Note: if you have blomstra/database-queue installed, it has its own Retries setting — the lower of the two values will effectively apply. Recommended to set both extension settings to the same value.
settings.queue_name_help: The named queue digest jobs are pushed onto. Must match the --queue argument in your queue:work cron. Default: digest.
settings.limit_members_help: Maximum number of new members to show in each digest.
settings.hot_reply_weight_help: How much each reply counts toward a discussion's hotness score. Higher = more replies-driven.
settings.hot_recency_weight_help: How much recency affects hotness. Higher = newer posts score more strongly. Set to 0 to rank by replies only.
settings.featured_discussion_help: Enter a discussion ID to pin it as a featured discussion at the top of every digest. Leave blank to show no featured discussion.
settings.send_hour_help: The time of day your digest emails will start sending.
settings.send_window_start_help: The time of day your digest emails will start sending. Choose a quiet period when your forum has low activity — typically late night or early morning.
settings.send_window_end_help: Leave this the same as the start time to send all emails at once. Set it later to spread sending across several hours — recommended for larger forums. For example, a 2 a.m. start and 4 a.m. end means emails go out gradually between 2 and 4 a.m.
settings.timezone_help: Choose your own timezone — not your server's. The send time you set below will fire at that hour in whichever timezone you select here. For example, if you're in Chicago and want emails sent at 8 a.m. your time, select Central Time and set the send time to 8 a.m.
settings.weekly_day_help: Day of the week on which weekly digests are sent.
settings.monthly_day_help: Day of the month on which monthly digests are sent. Capped at 28 so February always works.

frequency.help: Choose which digest frequency options are available to users on their settings page. Disabled options are hidden from the frequency selector.
frequency.daily_desc: Users can opt in to receive a digest every day. Best for high-traffic forums. Off by default.
frequency.weekly_desc: Users can opt in to receive a weekly digest. Recommended for most forums.
frequency.monthly_desc: Users can opt in to receive a monthly digest. Good for low-traffic or announcement-focused forums.

integrations.help: Control which optional extension integrations are included in digest emails. A toggle is only activatable when the required extension is installed and enabled. Enabled integrations appear in the Digest Order tab.
integrations.toggle_install_first_title: Install and enable the extension first
integrations.leaderboard_desc: Show the top members leaderboard in each digest, including rank changes, points earned during the period, and a biggest-mover callout.
integrations.badges_desc: Show badges earned during the period, the most-awarded badge, and the rarest badge awarded.
integrations.pickem_desc: Show upcoming pick'em matches, recent results, and the pick'em leaderboard.
integrations.picks_desc: Show upcoming CFB games, recent results, and the picks leaderboard. Supports confidence mode and week/season/all-time leaderboard scopes.
integrations.gamepedia_desc: Show the most discussed game pages and newly added games from Gamepedia.
integrations.resofire_gamepedia_desc: Show the most discussed games, newly added games, and top genres from Resofire Gamepedia.
integrations.reactions_desc: Use fof/reactions data for the Favorite Discussions section. When enabled, shows a per-reaction emoji breakdown instead of a plain like count. Thumbsdown and Confused reactions are excluded from scoring.
integrations.awards_desc: Show active and upcoming awards in the digest — including banner image, voting deadline countdown, category list, vote totals, and current front-runners when live votes are enabled.
integrations.favorites_auto_note_before: The following section is enabled automatically based on your active extensions and cannot be toggled here. To disable it, turn off
integrations.favorites_auto_note_after: or set its limit to 0 in Content Limits above.
integrations.favorites_desc: Requires flarum/likes. Shows the most-liked discussions from the digest period. If fof/reactions is also enabled above, shows a per-reaction emoji breakdown instead. To remove Favorites from the digest entirely, disable flarum/likes or set the limit to 0.
integrations.favorites_status_both: flarum/likes + fof/reactions active — showing reaction breakdown
integrations.favorites_status_likes: flarum/likes is active — showing like counts
integrations.favorites_status_disabled: flarum/likes is disabled — section will not appear in digest

schedule.window_spread: Digest emails will go out gradually from {from} to {to}. Subscribers are emailed in batches — your server stays responsive and no single minute carries the full load.
schedule.window_single: All digest emails will begin sending at {from}. Best for smaller forums with under 2,000 subscribers.

order.help: Use the arrows to set the order sections appear in the digest email. Only enabled integration sections appear here — enable them in the Settings tab first.
order.no_sections_active: No sections active. Enable integrations in the Settings tab.

token_checker.heading: Token Checker
token_checker.help: Paste an unsubscribe token to verify it is valid. Use this to confirm a user's token is intact without sending an email.

stats.no_send_history: No send history yet. History is recorded from this version onwards.

server.queue_name_help_1: The named queue digest jobs are pushed onto. Default:
server.queue_name_help_2: . If using the database queue, your
server.queue_name_help_3: cron must include this name. If using Horizon, this queue must be listed in your
server.crontab_notice_title: These lines must be added to your server's crontab
server.crontab_notice_body_1: They cannot be set from this panel. SSH into your server and run
server.crontab_notice_body_3: with the user that owns your Flarum files.
server.crontab_li_unsure_2: in your Flarum directory and check the owner column.
server.cron_scheduler_only_label: Flarum Scheduler — the only cron line you need
server.sync_no_setup_title: No worker setup required for Sync
server.sync_no_setup_1b: driver processes jobs inline during the web request. No queue worker, no Supervisor, and no queue backend is needed. The scheduler cron line below is the only requirement.
server.sync_no_setup_2a: When the scheduler fires at your configured send time,
server.sync_no_setup_2b: runs, builds each email, and sends it directly in the same process.
server.sync_limits_title: Sync has limits — know when to upgrade
server.sync_limit_under50: — sync is fine, most servers handle it without issue
server.sync_limit_200: — regular timeouts, memory exhaustion on typical VPS hosting
server.sync_limit_500: — effectively broken; posts will fail or appear to hang
server.sync_upgrade_prompt: When you hit those limits, switch to Flarum's built-in database queue driver — no extension install required. Add this to your
server.sync_switch_db_2: tab above for the correct cron setup.
server.db_enable_title: Enable the database queue driver first
server.db_enable_1a: Flarum 2.x includes a database queue driver built into core — no extension install required. Add this to your
server.db_enable_2: Jobs are stored in your database and processed by a queue worker running via cron. Your forum stays responsive during sends — the web request dispatches jobs instantly and the worker drains them in the background.
server.worker_flags_1: processes digest jobs first, then other Flarum notifications.
server.worker_flags_2: stops the worker cleanly before the next cron fires.
server.retries_db_body: If a job fails — for example if your mail server is temporarily unavailable — it is automatically retried up to the number of times configured in Queue Settings above. Retries use exponential backoff: 30 seconds, then 60 seconds, then 120 seconds between attempts.
server.retries_failed_1: Permanently failed jobs land in your
server.retries_failed_2: table. You can inspect and retry them with
server.retries_redis_body: If a job fails, it is automatically retried up to the number of times configured in Queue Settings above, using exponential backoff: 30 seconds, then 60 seconds, then 120 seconds between attempts.
server.retries_failed_redis_1: Permanently failed jobs land in your
server.retries_failed_redis_2: table. Inspect and retry them with
server.send_window_db_1: Instead of sending all emails at a single hour, you can set a send window — for example 1 a.m. to 5 a.m. — in the Settings tab. The scheduler dispatches one chunk of subscribers per minute throughout the window, spreading database load steadily over time instead of hitting everything at once.
server.send_window_db_2: The extension tracks its own progress and stops automatically once all subscribers have been processed. No extra cron entries are needed.
server.send_window_redis_1: Set a send window in the Settings tab to spread sending across several hours. The scheduler dispatches one chunk per minute throughout the window, keeping server load low and steady.
server.send_window_redis_2: The extension tracks progress automatically and stops once all subscribers are processed.
server.send_window_horizon_1: Set a send window in the Settings tab to spread sending across several hours. The scheduler dispatches one chunk per minute throughout the window, keeping server load low and steady. Horizon workers drain each chunk in parallel as it arrives.
server.parallel_db_note: Add one cron line per additional worker. Each worker runs independently and pulls jobs off the shared queue. Start with one or two workers and add more only if your queue depth grows faster than workers drain it.
server.parallel_redis_note: Add one cron line per additional worker. Each connects to Redis independently and pulls jobs from the shared queue. Start with one or two and add more only if needed.
server.prepop_note: Pre-populates the queue with jobs before the send window opens so workers have no construction overhead when they start. Set this to fire 10 minutes before your configured send window start. For example, if your window starts at 2 a.m., set this to run at 1:50 a.m. The first number is the minute (50 = :50) and the second is the hour in 24-hour format (1 = 1 a.m.). Adjust both to match your setup.
server.prepop_horizon_note: Pre-populates the queue with jobs before the send window opens so Horizon workers have no construction overhead when they start. Set this to fire 10 minutes before your configured send window start. For example, if your window starts at 2 a.m., set this to run at 1:50 a.m. The first number is the minute (50 = :50) and the second is the hour in 24-hour format (1 = 1 a.m.). Adjust both to match your setup.
server.db_upgrade_redis_title: When to consider upgrading to Redis / Valkey
server.db_upgrade_redis_1: The database queue is reliable and sufficient for most forums. Consider upgrading to Redis or Valkey with Horizon when you have 50,000+ subscribers and need higher throughput or real-time queue monitoring. Switch to the
server.redis_installed_title: Redis / Valkey must already be installed and running on your server
server.redis_installed_1: This extension does not cover Redis or Valkey installation. Once your Redis or Valkey server is running and
server.redis_installed_2: is installed and configured, choose your worker approach below.
server.redis_how_1a: The scheduler fires every minute via cron. When your configured send time arrives,
server.redis_how_1b: dispatches jobs to Redis. A second cron worker runs every minute, connects to Redis, and drains jobs for up to 55 seconds before exiting cleanly. This approach requires no persistent processes or Supervisor.
server.redis_worker_flags_2: stops the worker cleanly before the next cron fires. The worker connects to Redis via
server.redis_worker_flags_3: and drains jobs for the full 55 seconds before exiting.
server.redis_upgrade_horizon_1: For high-traffic forums, Horizon gives you persistent workers, real-time queue monitoring, automatic scaling, and a built-in dashboard. Switch to the
server.redis_upgrade_horizon_2: tab above for full setup instructions.
server.horizon_recommended_title: Horizon is the recommended approach for Redis / Valkey
server.horizon_recommended_1a: Horizon runs as a persistent background process managed by Supervisor. It watches your queues continuously, processes jobs as they arrive, scales workers automatically, and provides a real-time dashboard at
server.horizon_recommended_1b: . Unlike cron-based workers, Horizon never misses a job and never needs to be manually restarted.
server.horizon_step1_run: Run this command from your Flarum root directory:
server.horizon_step1_enable_2: in your Flarum admin panel under Extensions.
server.horizon_step2_1: Add the following to your Flarum root
server.horizon_step2_3: queue must be listed here so Horizon processes digest jobs. The
server.horizon_step2_4: queue handles all other Flarum notifications.
server.horizon_step3_1: Create the Horizon Supervisor config file at
server.horizon_step3_adjust_2: to match the user that owns your Flarum files (
server.horizon_step3_adjust_3: , or your hosting account username depending on your setup).
server.horizon_step4_notice_title: One cron line only — do not add a queue:work line
server.horizon_step4_notice_1a: When using Horizon + Supervisor, Horizon is your persistent queue worker. It runs continuously and listens to the
server.horizon_step4_notice_1d: cron line on top would create competing workers and cause unpredictable behaviour.
server.horizon_step4_notice_2: The scheduler cron below is the only cron line you need.
server.horizon_step5_1b: . You should see Horizon's status shown as
server.horizon_step5_1d: listed at the bottom of the dashboard showing the
server.recommended_intro: These are starting-point recommendations. Actual performance depends on your server hardware, mail provider response times, and how many users have opted in to digests. Always monitor your queue depth and adjust accordingly.
server.recommended_tip: For 50,000+ member forums, consider switching from the built-in database queue driver to Redis or Valkey with Horizon and Supervisor for significantly higher throughput and real-time monitoring. Switch to the Redis / Valkey tab in the Cron Setup section above for full setup instructions. Send history is retained automatically: 30 daily entries, 52 weekly, 24 monthly.
server.tbl_r1_strategy: One worker, single-hour mode — all subscribers dispatched in one run
server.tbl_r2_strategy: One worker, single-hour or short window
server.tbl_r7_strategy: Ten+ workers, 4+ hour window, consider Redis + Supervisor

test_send.heading: Send Test Email
test_send.help: Send a live digest email immediately to any address. Uses your account's visibility to build content. Does not affect scheduled sends or unsubscribe tokens.
test_send.error_generic: Failed to send. Check your mail settings and try again.
test_send.theme_hint_light: Sends with light mode styles applied
test_send.theme_hint_dark: Sends with dark mode styles applied
test_send.theme_light_only: Light mode only — install and enable fof/nightmode to unlock dark mode emails

onboarding.mode_help: Controls how new users are enrolled in digest emails when their account is activated.
onboarding.mode_none: No default — users choose their own preference
onboarding.frequency_help: The digest frequency new users are automatically enrolled at. Only frequencies enabled in User Frequency Options above are available.
onboarding.no_frequencies_enabled: No frequencies are currently enabled. Enable at least one frequency in User Frequency Options above.

leaderboard.rankings_note: Rankings based on total points earned through posts, discussions, likes, badges, and daily logins.

pickem.upcoming_note: Don't miss your chance to pick — cutoff times below.

picks.confidence_note: Confidence mode is active — points are weighted by each player's confidence rating (1–10) on their picks.
picks.upcoming_note: Picks lock individually at each game's cutoff time.

awards.tallying: Results are being tallied. Stay tuned for the announcement!

footer.notice: You're receiving this because you're a member of {forum}. Your digest is currently set to {frequency}.

unsubscribe.heading: Email Digest Preferences
unsubscribe.greeting: Hi, {name}. Choose how often you'd like to receive a digest of what's happening in {forum}.
unsubscribe.click_hint: Click an option to save your preference instantly.
unsubscribe.daily_desc: One email per day with the latest activity.
unsubscribe.monthly_desc: A monthly summary on the {day} of each month.
unsubscribe.off_desc: Don't send me any digest emails.

unsubscribe_saved.heading: Preferences saved
unsubscribe_saved.body: Your digest email preferences have been updated successfully.

unsubscribe_invalid.heading: This link is no longer valid
unsubscribe_invalid.body_expired: This unsubscribe link has either expired (links are valid for 90 days) or has already been used. A fresh link is included in every digest email we send you.
unsubscribe_invalid.body_signin: To manage your digest preferences, sign in to {forum} and visit your notification settings.
